title: Hides, Skins and Leather Trade (Training Standards for Licensing Hides Dressers and Graders) Regulations, 2010
title_en: Hides, Skins and Leather Trade (Training Standards for Licensing Hides Dressers and Graders) Regulations, 2010

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Hides, Skins and Leather Trade (Training Standards for Licensing Hide Dressers and Graders) Regulations, 2010 and shall come into operation on the date of publication.

provisions.title: Applica­tion
provisions.content: These Regulations shall apply to all hide graders and dressers to be licensed under the Act .

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these Regulations unless the context otherwise requires— “ Act ” means the Hides, Skins and Leather Trade Act; “ Director ” means the Director responsible for Hides, Skins and Leather Development in the Ministry responsible for livestock development; “ Minister ” means the Minister responsible for Livestock Part II – Qualifications and institutions

provisions.title: Qualifica­tion for licensing a hide dresser
provisions.content: (1) Without prejudice to the provisions of Section 15 (1) of the Act , a hide dresser shall have the following qualifications— (a) a minimum of primary education and holder of a certificate in hide dressing from a recognized institution or its equivalent as approved by the Director ; (b) work experience in hide, skin or leather licensed premises for a period of not less than three months; (2) The areas of training for the purpose of licensing are— (a) general animal husbandry; (b) introduction to hides and skins ripping, flaying, defleshing, fleshing and lacing; (c) methods of curing hides and skins; (d) spoilage and preservation procedures; (e) storage procedures; (f) introduction to hide and skin diseases; (g) dressing tools and equipments; (h) any other areas as may be approved by the Director .

provisions.title: Qualifica­tion for licensing a hide grader
provisions.content: Without prejudice to the provisions of Section 16 (1) of the Act , a hide grader shall have the following qualifications— (a) a minimum of ordinary secondary education and holder of a certificate in hide grading From a recognized institution or its equivalent as approved by the Director ; (b) work experience in hide, skin or leather licensed premises for a period not less than three months; (2) The areas of training for the purpose of grading are— (a) general animal husbandry; (b) introduction to hides, skins and leather; (c) methods of curing hides and skins; (d) hides and skins defects control; (e) principals of grading hides, skins and leather; (f) storage procedures and transportation; (g) grading tools and equipments; (h) any other areas as may be approved by the Director .

provisions.title: Recog­nized institu­tions
provisions.content: (1) The Director shall approve the training institutions and curricula for dressing and grading of hides, skins and leather. (2) Subject to sub-regulation (1) the following institutions are recognized to offer training on dressing of hides, skins and leather: (a) livestock training institutes; and (b) Vocational Education Training Authority institutes registered and licensed to offer training in livestock allied subjects.
